title: iris tenax subsp. innominata
intro: Iris tenax subsp. innominata, also known as the Rocky Mountain Iris, is a subspecies of the Iris tenax plant, which is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's root is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as being used to treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the Rocky Mountain Iris often involve drying and processing the root into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
